Sorbus aucuparia, or Mountain Ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Asia, known for its white flowers and red berries. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Sorbus aucuparia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
